body.title: [sprint] Editor-isolation smoke test — can devstral@Hex serialize a known-good plan into Edit calls at all?
body.intention: I intend that we observe the output half of the coding pipeline (plan -> serialized Edit calls) working at least once before investing further in the input half, because across every regime tried DS.0 has made zero edits and we have never confirmed which half is broken.
body.description:
**Problem (the Fable-consult insight):** across BOTH confirm-by runs devstral made 0 Write/Edit attempts. Run 1 we attributed to orientation-drowning; run 2 to the Hex timeout. But that attribution is UNTESTED — it is equally consistent with devstral being unable to serialize exact-string Edit calls under this ACI at all. The entire OUTPUT half of the pipeline (plan -> Edit/Write calls) has never been observed to work. Delta's 51%/89% numbers are input-token reductions, not task completions — they prove the packet shrinks input, not that DS.0 can finish. We have been optimizing the input side of a pipeline whose output side has never once been seen to work.

**Proposed shape:** a diagnostic harness that BYPASSES the architect entirely. Hand-write a 2-3 edit plan for an already-closed real ticket (a known-good change we can verify) and feed it straight into the EDITOR half — EDITOR_PROMPT + full-toolset AgenticLoop(aci_mode=True) — on devstral@Hex. This isolates the single unanswered question: can devstral-24b turn a known-good plan into successful Edit calls, given ACI windowed reads?

**Affected files:** a scratch harness (scratchpad) that constructs the editor AgenticLoop directly (or a thin standalone entry into ArchitectEditorFlow's editor phase); no production behavior change expected — this is a probe.

**Design rules:** log the crossing; NO SQLITE; **LIVE Hex run -> the dual push-guard is REQUIRED before firing** (coding.md still targets main): fail-closed .git/hooks/pre-push + bogus push URL, verified BLOCKING before the fire; restore + clean-tree + reachable-origin verify after. Build-core-only, no close from the minion.

**Scope boundary:** IN — determine whether devstral@Hex can serialize a known-good plan into successful Edit calls, and record the funnel. OUT — the architect, the build packet, and FIXING a broken edit interface (that becomes the next ticket if this fails).

**Test plan / completion:** observe evidence — nonzero Edit/Write attempts AND the edit-success ratio ('OK: edited' vs 'old_string not found' / 'matches N locations' from _tool_edit). Two verdicts both count as done: (PASS) editor applies the plan -> the build-packet trunk (T-uu-build-packet-compiler) is validated to build behind it; (FAIL) editor cannot serialize edits -> the real bug is the edit interface/ACI or per-role tiering, and the roadmap pivots THERE, not to more input-side work. Either outcome yields the decisive signal.

**Completion criteria:** a recorded funnel for the hand-written plan — plan given -> edits attempted (count) -> edits applied (success/fail count) -> tests run -> result — with the explicit verdict (editor viable / editor is the bug). This is a diagnostic/observation ticket with no red->green behavior change of its own -> closes shipped-unproven, naming the observed verdict as the lever.

**Rollback:** harness is scratch-only; nothing to roll back.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
